evals.prompt: Write a user story for password reset via email for a web app, then break it into developer tasks. The tech stack is Node.js with Express and PostgreSQL.
evals.expected_output: A user story in As a / I want / So that format with acceptance criteria in Given/When/Then format, followed by a table of developer tasks with effort estimates and file hints.
expectations: Story uses 'As a / I want / So that' format
Acceptance criteria use 'Given / When / Then' format
At least one AC covers the error scenario (unregistered email or expired link)
Developer tasks are in a table with at least 4 rows
Each task row includes an effort estimate (XS/S/M/L or similar)
At least one task row includes a file path or module hint
Story includes a story points estimate

evals.prompt: Create user stories for a shopping cart feature in a React + Python FastAPI e-commerce app. Users should be able to add items, update quantities, remove items, and proceed to checkout.
evals.expected_output: Multiple user stories (one per action or grouped logically) with acceptance criteria and developer task breakdowns.
expectations: Output contains at least 2 separate user stories
Each story has its own acceptance criteria section
Stories cover at least add-to-cart and checkout or remove-from-cart scenarios
Developer tasks reference relevant frontend and backend files or modules
Each story has a story points estimate
